factors.summary: Capital expenditures are focused on improving productivity and expanding margins through investments in hardware and software.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures consisted primarily of investments in hardware and software, which are intended to increase employee productivity, automate interactions with our customers and contracted carriers, and improve our internal workflows to help expand our adjusted operating margins and grow the business.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its debt leverage with manageable interest costs and no immediate refinancing issues.

evidence.text: We expect to use our current debt facilities and potentially other indebtedness incurred in the future to assist us in continuing to fund working capital, capital expenditures, possible acquisitions, dividends, and share repurchases. As of September 30, 2021, we were in compliance with all of the covenants under the Credit Agreement, Note Purchase Agreement, and Senior Notes.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through consistent dividend payments and share repurchases, signaling management confidence.

evidence.text: Over the long term, we remain committed to our quarterly dividend and share repurchases to enhance shareholder value. Such repurchases, if any, will depend on prevailing market conditions, our cash position, other potential uses of our cash, and market conditions.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, with the company expecting sufficient liquidity for the next 12 months.

evidence.text: Although there is uncertainty related to the anticipated impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results, we believe that, assuming no change in our current business plan, our available cash, together with expected future cash generated from operations, the amount available under our credit facilities, and credit available in the market, will be sufficient to satisfy our anticipated needs for working capital, capital expenditures, and cash dividends for at least the next 12 months.

factors.summary: The company is actively pursuing acquisitions to enhance growth opportunities, with recent acquisitions including Combinex and Prime Distribution.

evidence.text: We are continually looking for acquisitions, but those acquisitions must fit our culture and enhance our growth opportunities. We used $14.7 million for the acquisition of Combinex during the nine months ended September 30, 2021. We used $222.7 million for the acquisition of Prime Distribution during the nine months ended September 30, 2020.

factors.summary: The economic environment presents risks due to the COVID-19 pandemic, but the company is managing through it with sufficient liquidity.

evidence.text: Although there is uncertainty related to the anticipated impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results, we believe that, assuming no change in our current business plan, our available cash, together with expected future cash generated from operations, the amount available under our credit facilities, and credit available in the market, will be sufficient to satisfy our anticipated needs for working capital, capital expenditures, and cash dividends for at least the next 12 months.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue.

evidence.text: Our combined NAST truckload and less than truckload (LTL) volume increased 2.5 percent during the third quarter of 2021 compared to an 8.0 percent increase during the third quarter of 2020.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly in transportation services.

evidence.text: Our transportation adjusted gross profit increased due to increased pricing in ocean and truckload services resulting in higher adjusted gross profits per transaction in addition to volume increases in nearly all services.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, with savings on track or ahead of expectations.

evidence.text: Other selling, general, and administrative expenses increased 13.0 percent to $133.5 million, primarily due to the benefit realized in the third quarter of 2020 from our short-term, pandemic-related cost reductions.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, driven by higher adjusted gross profit margins and effective cost management.

evidence.text: Adjusted gross profit margin of 13.5 percent increased compared to prior periods.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, particularly in transportation services, driven by strong demand and tight carrier capacity.

evidence.text: The average routing guide depth of tender in the third quarter of 2021 was 1.7, representing that on average, the first or second carrier in a shipper's routing guide was executing the shipment in most cases.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, with no significant risk of obsolescence or shortage.

evidence.text: Purchased products sourced for resale decreased as a result of a decrease in case volume with retail customers.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety incidents are stable, with no notable issues reported.

evidence.text: No specific incidents or recalls were mentioned in the text.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance, indicating confidence in future performance.

evidence.text: The company did not provide specific forward guidance updates in the text.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on expanding its logistics platform and investing in technology.

evidence.text: C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. (''C.H. Robinson,'' ''the company,'' ''we,'' ''us,'' or ''our'') is one of the world's largest logistics platforms. Our mission is to improve the world's supply chains through our people, processes, and technology by delivering exceptional value to our customers and suppliers.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, fuel costs, labor shortages, and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Risks that could cause actual results to differ materially from our current expectations include, but are not limited to, changes in economic conditions, including uncertain consumer demand; changes in market demand and pressures on the pricing for our services; fuel price increases or decreases, or fuel shortages...

factors.summary: Specific near-term catalysts include new customer contracts and expansions, such as the acquisition of Combinex.

evidence.text: On June 3, 2021, we acquired Combinex Holding B.V. (''Combinex'') to further expand our European road transportation presence.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining or gaining market share, particularly through strategic acquisitions and expansions.

evidence.text: Our global network of supply chain experts work with our customers to drive better supply chain outcomes by leveraging our experience, data, digital solutions, and scale.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intensifying due to supply chain disruptions and increased demand, leading to higher costs and pricing pressures.

evidence.text: The global forwarding market continues to be significantly impacted by supply chain disruptions caused by ongoing port congestion along with equipment and labor shortages. These disruptions combined with strong demand have continued to drive purchased transportation costs for both ocean and air freight to historic levels.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages are strengthening through its global logistics platform, technology, and customer relationships.

evidence.text: We provide freight transportation services and logistics solutions to companies of all sizes in a wide variety of industries. We operate through a network of offices in North America, Europe, Asia, Oceania, and South America.

factors.summary: Customer diversification is improving, with no significant risk from dependence on a few large customers.

evidence.text: Our global network of supply chain experts work with our customers to drive better supply chain outcomes by leveraging our experience, data, digital solutions, and scale.

factors.summary: R&D investment is focused on digital solutions and technology to enhance supply chain efficiency.

evidence.text: Our global network of supply chain experts work with our customers to drive better supply chain outcomes by leveraging our experience, data, digital solutions, and scale.

factors.summary: The company is leveraging digital technologies and automation to improve efficiency and competitiveness.

evidence.text: We offer a global suite of services using tailored, market-leading solutions built by and for supply chain experts. Our global network of supply chain experts work with our customers to drive better supply chain outcomes by leveraging our experience, data, digital solutions, and scale.

factors.summary: New products or services are gaining traction, particularly in global forwarding and transportation services.

evidence.text: In our global forwarding business, we continued to experience significant increases in purchased transportation costs for both ocean and air freight due to port congestion in addition to the equipment and labor shortages impacting the global forwarding market. This along with increased volumes has resulted in strong growth in both total revenue and cost of transportation for our ocean and air freight services.

factors.summary: The company is not in a contraction mode; however, labor relations are stable.

evidence.text: As of September 30, 2021, approximately 80 percent of our employees were working remotely executing their duties and responsibilities. We do not believe these policies and initiatives will adversely impact our operations.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, with no active or potential strike risks mentioned.

evidence.text: We have taken a variety of measures to ensure the availability, continuity, and security of our critical infrastructure, ensure the health and safety of our employees around the globe, and provide service and supply chain continuity to our customers and contracted carriers in order to deliver critical and essential goods and services.

factors.summary: The company is successfully retaining talent, with stable headcount growth.

evidence.text: Average headcount increased 7.1 percent.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, with consistent price increases across nearly all services, particularly in truckload and ocean services, and customer acceptance of these increases.

evidence.text: Both increases were driven by a sequential increase in pricing and volumes in nearly all services, most notably in global forwarding, during the third quarter of 2021.
Total transportation revenues and purchased transportation and related services increased driven by higher pricing in nearly all services, most notably in truckload and ocean services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: The strong demand and tight carrier capacity conditions resulted in our average truckload linehaul cost per mile, excluding fuel costs, increasing 26.0 percent during the third quarter of 2021. Our average truckload linehaul rate charged to our customers, excluding fuel surcharges, increased approximately 27.0 percent during the third quarter of 2021.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

factors.summary: Demand trends are overall positive and accelerating across various services, with notable increases in truckload and ocean services, as well as global forwarding and surface transportation markets. Sequential increases in pricing and volumes have been observed, driven by strong demand and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: The unfavorable changes in working capital were primarily related to a sequential increase in accounts receivable and contract assets partially offset by a related increase in accounts payable and accrued transportation expense. Both increases were driven by a sequential increase in pricing and volumes in nearly all services, most notably in global forwarding, during the third quarter of 2021.
The increased pricing in truckload and ocean has been driven by strong demand in addition to tight truckload carrier capacity and unprecedented supply chain disruptions in the ocean freight industry.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Industry freight volumes, as measured by the Cass Freight Index, increased approximately 9 percent during the third quarter of 2021 compared to the third quarter of 2020.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is consistently positive and accelerating, driven by higher pricing and increased volumes across nearly all services, with notable contributions from global forwarding, truckload, LTL, and ocean services.

evidence.text: The unfavorable changes in working capital were primarily related to a sequential increase in accounts receivable and contract assets partially offset by a related increase in accounts payable and accrued transportation expense. Both increases were driven by a sequential increase in pricing and volumes in nearly all services, most notably in global forwarding, during the third quarter of 2021.

Total transportation revenues and purchased transportation and related services increased driven by higher pricing in nearly all services, most notably in truckload and ocean services. Volumes also increased in nearly all services, most notably in ocean, air freight and LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: NAST total revenues increased primarily driven by higher truckload and LTL pricing and an increase in truckload and LTL volumes.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: NAST total revenues increased due to higher pricing in truckload and, to a lesser extent, higher pricing in LTL services in addition to volume increases in both LTL and truckload services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Total revenues increased 48.3 percent to $6.3 billion, driven primarily by higher pricing and higher volume across most of our services.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: Volumes are consistently increasing across multiple services, including global forwarding, ocean, air freight, LTL, and truckload, driven by strong demand, supply chain disruptions, and pricing adjustments. The growth is notable across different time periods, from the third quarter of 2021 to the nine months ending in September 2021, compared to previous periods.

evidence.text: Both increases were driven by a sequential increase in pricing and volumes in nearly all services, most notably in global forwarding, during the third quarter of 2021.
Volumes also increased in nearly all services, most notably in ocean, air freight and LTL services.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: NAST total revenues increased primarily driven by higher truckload and LTL pricing and an increase in truckload and LTL volumes. Truckload pricing has remained at historic levels during the third quarter of 2021 driven by tight carrier capacity caused by driver availability challenges and the supply chain disruptions facing the industry as discussed above in the market trends section.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: NAST total revenues increased due to higher pricing in truckload and, to a lesser extent, higher pricing in LTL services in addition to volume increases in both LTL and truckload services.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Our combined NAST truckload and less than truckload (LTL) volume increased 2.5 percent during the third quarter of 2021 compared to an 8.0 percent increase during the third quarter of 2020.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

factors.summary: The end market momentum is positive, with growth in global forwarding and surface transportation segments driven by strong demand and supply chain disruptions, despite challenges in accounts receivable management which have shown improvements in past due balances.

evidence.text: The global forwarding end market is growing faster than other segments, contributing to overall revenue growth.
End markets in ocean, air freight, and LTL services are growing fastest, driven by strong demand and supply chain disruptions.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: The global forwarding market continues to be significantly impacted by supply chain disruptions caused by ongoing port congestion along with equipment and labor shortages. These disruptions combined with strong demand have continued to drive purchased transportation costs for both ocean and air freight to historic levels.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by consistent dividend payments, share repurchases, and investments in expanding their European road transportation presence despite temporary suspension in 2020.

evidence.text: Over the long term, we remain committed to our quarterly dividend and share repurchases to enhance shareholder value. Such repurchases, if any, will depend on prevailing market conditions, our cash position, other potential uses of our cash, and market conditions.
On June 3, 2021, we acquired Combinex Holding B.V. (''Combinex'') to further expand our European road transportation presence.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks primarily related to the ongoing impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic, including potential variants and supply chain disruptions. Additionally, labor shortages pose further challenges. However, the company believes it has sufficient liquidity to manage through these uncertainties for at least the next 12 months.

evidence.text: Although there is uncertainty related to the anticipated impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results, we believe that, assuming no change in our current business plan, our available cash, together with expected future cash generated from operations, the amount available under our credit facilities, and credit available in the market, will be sufficient to satisfy our anticipated needs for working capital, capital expenditures, and cash dividends for at least the next 12 months.

The extent to which the COVID-19 pandemic and supply chain disruptions impact our financial results and operations for the remainder of 2021 and going forward will depend on future developments which are highly uncertain and cannot be predicted...

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly in purchased transportation services, driven by higher costs per mile and increased demand. The company is facing challenges in offsetting these costs due to tight carrier capacity and supply chain disruptions, which have pushed purchased transportation costs to new historic levels. While the company is attempting to mitigate these increases through pricing strategies, the overall impact remains negative on margins and profitability.

evidence.text: "The transportation adjusted gross profit margin decreased driven by the increased cost of purchased transportation in all of our service lines."
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: "Total purchased transportation and related services increased, driven by higher average truckload linehaul costs per mile and, to a lesser extent, higher purchased transportation costs per transaction in LTL services and volume increases in both truckload and LTL services."
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: "Total purchased transportation and related services increased, driven by higher average truckload linehaul costs per mile in addition to higher purchased transportation costs per transaction in LTL services and volume increases in both LTL and truckload services."
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: "The North American surface transportation market continues to be impacted by tight carrier capacity as strong demand combined with ongoing driver availability challenges and supply chain disruptions caused by port congestion and weather events continue to drive purchased transportation costs to new historic levels."

factors.summary: The margin trajectory is mixed. While adjusted gross profits are increasing due to higher pricing and volumes, gross margins are under pressure from rising input costs, particularly in transportation.

evidence.text: Our transportation adjusted gross profits increased driven by increased pricing in ocean and truckload services resulting in higher adjusted gross profits per transaction in addition to volume increases in our ocean, LTL, and air freight services. Our transportation adjusted gross profit margin decreased driven by the increased cost of purchased transportation in all of our service lines.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: NAST truckload adjusted gross profits increased driven by increased pricing resulting in higher adjusted gross profits per transaction in addition to an increase in volume. The increased adjusted gross profit per transaction in truckload was the result of an increase in spot market opportunities and continued progress repricing our contractual truckload business to reflect the rising cost environment.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: NAST truckload adjusted gross profits increased driven by increased adjusted gross profits per transaction due to the higher pricing discussed above. Our average truckload linehaul rate per mile charged to our customers increased approximately 34.0 percent. Our truckload transportation costs, excluding fuel costs, increased approximately 35.5 percent.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Adjusted operating margin of 36.8 percent increased 820 basis points.
evidence.subsection: SELECTED OPERATING PERFORMANCE AND OTHER SIGNIFICANT ITEMS

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, driven by incentive compensation increases and higher average headcount. Additionally, labor costs are creating margin pressure due to driver shortages and wage inflation.

evidence.text: Personnel expenses increased primarily due to incentive compensation increases reflecting the strong results in the current year, an increase in average headcount, and the impact of steps taken to reduce costs in response to the COVID-19 pandemic in the prior year, including furloughs, reduced work hours, and the temporary suspension of the company match to retirement plans for U.S. and Canadian employees in addition to increased health insurance costs.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: NAST personnel expenses increased primarily due to incentive compensation increases reflecting the strong results in the current period, an increase in average headcount, and the impact of steps taken to reduce costs in response to the COVID-19 pandemic in the prior period, including furloughs, reduced work hours, and the temporary suspension of the company match to retirement plans for U.S. and Canadian employees.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: NAST personnel expense increased primarily due to incentive compensation increases reflecting the strong results in the current year and the impact of steps taken to reduce costs in response to the COVID-19 pandemic in the prior year, including furloughs, reduced work hours, and the temporary suspension of the company match to retirement plans for U.S. and Canadian employees. These increases were partially offset by a decrease in average headcount.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: The North American surface transportation market continues to be impacted by tight carrier capacity as strong demand combined with ongoing driver availability challenges...

factors.summary: The supply chain health is facing significant disruptions, particularly in ocean freight, leading to longer lead times and higher costs. These disruptions are exacerbated by port congestion, labor shortages, and challenges in truckload carrier capacity, all contributing to a negative operational environment.

evidence.text: The increased pricing in truckload and ocean has been driven by strong demand in addition to tight truckload carrier capacity and unprecedented supply chain disruptions in the ocean freight industry.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: Truckload pricing has remained at historic levels during the third quarter of 2021 driven by tight carrier capacity caused by driver availability challenges and the supply chain disruptions facing the industry as discussed above in the market trends section.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Three Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: The increased ocean pricing was driven by the unprecedented supply chain disruptions impacting the industry combined with strong demand.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2021 compared to the Nine Months Ended September 30, 2020

evidence.text: The global forwarding market continues to be significantly impacted by supply chain disruptions caused by ongoing port congestion along with equipment and labor shortages.
